revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun]][[ja:八幡観音塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun from the Kofun period, designated as a National History Site of Japan since 1948}} {{Distinguish| Kannonzuka Kofun}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun | native_name =八幡観音塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yawata Kannonzuka Kofun, Funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.34016667|N|138.94008333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takasaki|qid=Q336438|1=Takasaki}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1973 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Museum nearby) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun'''|八幡観音塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hachiman neighborhood the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1948.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204953|title=観音塚古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> The site dates from the late 6th century AD, and many of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} discovered during the [[archaeological excavation]] were designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 1961<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/168685|title=上野国八幡観音塚古墳出土品 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Hachiman Kannonzuka Kofun is located on a plateau sandwiched between the {{ill|Karasu River (Gunma)|en|Karasu River (Gunma)|qid=Q3915532|lt=Karasugawa River|1=Karasu River (Gunma)}} and the Usui River, northwest of the city of Takasaki. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Its main dimensions are:
;Total length: 105 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 105 meters wide x 14 meters high, 4-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 14 meters high, 3-tiers
The tumulus is built in four tiers in the rectangular portion and three tiers for the circular portion. Unusually, the width of the rectangular portion is larger than the circular portion. The tumulus was at least partially surrounded by a moat. The 15.8 meter long horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is made from stone megaliths, some measuring 4.5 x 3.4 meters, weighing over 50 tons, orientated to the southwest. The size of the megaliths used to make this chamber is also unusual, and the tomb is comparable to the [[Ishibutai Kofun]] in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|qid=Q752397|lt=Asuka|1=Asuka, Nara}}. The burial chamber was found to be intact when opened in March 1945 by locals who were building a {{ill|Bomb shelter|en|Bomb shelter|qid=Q17003536|lt=Bomb shelter|1=Bomb shelter}} and some 300 artifacts were discovered within. These grave goods included four [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirrors]], gold, silver and glass jewelry, iron swords, iron spearheads, fragments of armor, horse harnesses, sa11les, stirrups and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, and other containers of earthenware and metal. Two of the bronze mirrors were identical to those found in the {{ill|Inariyama Kofun|en|Inariyama Kofun|qid=Q3198272|lt=Inariyama Kofun|1=Inariyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}} and must have been cast using the same mould. From these artifacts, the tumulus has been dated to the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century AD, and may have been one of the last of the great keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' to have been built in Gunma.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The recovered artifacts were found to be in good preservation, and many are displayed at the nearby {{nihongo|'''Kannonzuka Archaeological Museum'''|観音塚考古資料館|Kannonzuka kōko shiryōkan}}. It is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Gumma-Yawata Station|en|Gumma-Yawata Station|qid=Q899937|lt=Gumma-Yawata Station|1=Gumma-Yawata Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|qid=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line|1=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> 八幡観音塚古墳羨道.jpg|Entry to Burial Chamber 八幡観音塚古墳玄室.jpg|Inside Burial Chamber Takasaki City Kannonduka Museum of Archaeololgy.JPG|Kannonzuka Museum of Archaeology </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|qid=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== [[Category:Yawata Kannonzuka Kofun}} *[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2013121600217/ Takasaki City official site] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.takasaki.gunma.jp/docs/2014010800114/ Takasaki Kannonzuka Museum of Archeological Documents] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
